title: Is Wolfspeed Stock Headed Toward $0 Again?
text: Wolfspeed's stock has surged nearly 170% this year following its emergence from bankruptcy with reduced debt and new leadership. However, significant operational challenges persist. The company continues to struggle with negative gross margins and cash flow problems that plagued it before bankruptcy. In fiscal Q3, Wolfspeed recorded a gross margin of -27%, meaning it sells components below production cost. Even adjusted figures show only a 4.6% margin after accounting for manufacturing underutilization. The silicon carbide chip manufacturer faces yield issues with its 200-millimeter wafers, still attempting to prove reliability to customers. Additionally, revenue declined 19% year-over-year to $150.2 million, partly because the anticipated electric vehicle market hasn't materialized as expected. Cash burn remains concerning, with negative operating cash flow of $84 million in the quarter. The company holds $1.2 billion in cash against $1.7 billion in debt. A recent balance sheet restructuring involving convertible notes and warrant redemptions will save approximately $62 million annually in interest expenses. Looking forward, management guided fiscal Q4 revenue between $140-$160 million, down from $197 million previously. While bankruptcy appears unlikely in the near term given current cash reserves, the analyst concludes the fundamental business challenges suggest this is a stock to avoid.
